summary: The County of San Mateo Technology Services Department (TSD) is seeking proposals from qualified vendors to assess, configure, and implement Microsoft's Identity & Access Management (IAM) solution to support the County's identity lifecycle management across its workforce. The technical scope encompasses integration with Workday as the system of record and ServiceNow for account provisioning, automated onboarding/offboarding workflows, cross-domain workforce transfers, SAML SSO integration for external partners, migration to Microsoft Entra SSO with seamless MFA transition, and automated account expiration for seasonal, temporary, and contractor workforce members. The contract is structured as a 36-month base period with two 12-month renewal options, with an estimated value of $300,000 to $500,000 funded by County sources, and proposals are evaluated using a best-value approach across two phases — technical proposal evaluation (45% method/approach, 30% experience, 25% price) followed by live demonstrations and interviews for elevated proposals. Proposers must have a minimum of three years' experience implementing the requested solution and at least three successful government agency implementations of similar size within the last five years, along with active SAM.gov registration and comprehensive insurance coverage including $5,000,000 cyber liability. This initiative is strategically significant as it modernizes the County's identity governance infrastructure, enabling automated lifecycle communications, role-based access controls, approval escalation workflows, and security group schema integration across integrated and third-party applications to reduce manual intervention by TSD staff and improve security posture.
periodOfPerformance: 36 months base + 2 options of 12 months each
title: Implementation of Microsoft's Identity & Access Management (IAM) Solution
placeOfPerformance: 455 County Center, 3rd Fl, Redwood City, CA 94063
